audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences are safe: (1) euid/egid/peerbv parameters are always passed as stack addresses (&uid, &gid, &peerbv) by both callers (daemon.c:2268, nssov.c:301). (2) cmsg at L129 is computed from CMSG_FIRSTHDR before recvmsg with msg.msg_controllen=sizeof(cmsghdr)+sizeof(int), guaranteeing non-NULL. (3) cmp at L199 lacks a null check for the 0<controllen<sizeof(cmsghdr) edge case, but on BSD the kernel (not the peer) provides SCM_CREDS and would never return a sub-header-size partial control message; the existing checks for msg.msg_controllen==0 and MSG_CTRUNC cover all reachable null cases.
